channel.title: The Grant Desk - Visual & Media Arts, EU
channel.description: Paid open calls, fellowships and residencies in AI, tech, research, and digital and mixed-media arts, sorted into the right pile. Updated as new calls land on the desk. Filtered to: Visual & Media Arts, EU.

item.title: Open Call 2027: Galerie mladych (Galerie TIC Brno)
item.description:
Organisation: Galerie TIC Brno

Location: Brno, Czech Republic. Exhibition projects realized at Galerie mladych during 2027.

Award: The gallery covers the financial costs of exhibition installation, an artist/curator fee, collection and transport of the exhibition, and basic PR (photodocumentation included).

Duration: Exhibition project for the year 2027.

Deadline: 20 Jul 2026

Galerie TIC announces an open call for exhibition projects for Galerie mladych (Gallery of Young Artists) for the year 2027. Applicants should send a completed application form, a project description with visual documentation, and an artist's portfolio with CV by midnight on 20 July 2026 via the online form. THE GALLERY OFFERS: to cover the financial costs of exhibition installation; an artist/curator fee; collection and transport of the exhibition; and basic PR (photodocumentation included). APPLICATION: the application form, a short description and visual documentation of the artistic/curatorial project, and a portfolio with CV. Results will be published on the gallery's website and social media no later than the end of August 2026. Deadline 20 July 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Prisma Art Prize 20th Edition
item.description:
Organisation: Prisma Art Prize

Location: Rome, Italy

Award: EUR 2,000 annual cash fund distributed across quarterly winners, plus group exhibition at Palazzo Merulana, inclusion in the collective catalogue, artist interviews, and eligibility for solo show prizes (Isorrophia Gallery, Pallavicini-Dettori) and partner art residencies (Dar Meso, Casacon, Osservatoriomaree) EUR 34 entry fee for 1 to 3 artworks, plus EUR 5 for each additional artwork up to a maximum of 8

Duration: Group exhibition runs November 5 to 8, 2026

Deadline: 10 Sep 2026

The Prisma Art Prize is an international award for painting and two-dimensional artistic research based in Rome. The 20th edition selects emerging and established artists working in painting, drawing, illustration, printmaking, and traditional mixed media for a group exhibition at Palazzo Merulana, opening November 5, 2026. Winners share an annual cash fund of EUR 2,000 distributed across quarterly calls and receive inclusion in the collective catalogue plus artist interviews. Additional opportunities include solo exhibition prizes and fully funded placements at partner art residencies. The prize is open internationally to all living artists regardless of age, gender, nationality, or educational background. The submission deadline for this edition is September 10, 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Artist Residency Autumn 2026 (Centre for Multispecies Relations and Future Ecologies, Palermo)
item.description:
Organisation: Centre for Multispecies Relations and Future Ecologies

Location: Altarello di Baida district, Palermo, Sicily, Italy. On-site 62-day residency, 12 October - 12 December 2026. Open to all nationalities, but applicants must be officially residing in a Creative Europe country (excluding Italy).

Award: Total allowance of EUR 1,860 (before tax) per artist, plus a travel budget of EUR 400 per artist (for distances under 4,000 km) with a EUR 400 green-mobility top-up, plus a production budget. Also provided: on-site accommodation in a fully furnished independent house (private bedroom; shared kitchen, living room, two bathrooms; outdoor studio space 100m from the working site), access to recycled/salvaged materials through the Centre's partnerships, a bicycle per resident, and a final public event.

Duration: 62-day residency, 12 October - 12 December 2026, structured in five phases (orientation, observation/fieldwork, proposal and ecological feasibility, creation, and public restitution).

Deadline: Closed 05 Jul 2026

The Centre for Multispecies Relations and Future Ecologies invites applications from two artists for a 62-day residency (12 October - 12 December 2026) at its site in the Altarello di Baida district of Palermo, Sicily. The residency is structured around a 1,500 sqm garden at the threshold between the city's urban expansion and the historic rural landscape of the surrounding hills; the site, degraded through previous use (soil compaction and significant biodiversity loss), functions as both studio and subject, and artists participate in an ongoing process of ecological transformation, producing interventions that emerge from extended observation and fieldwork. Applications are welcome across all mediums and disciplines (installation, sound, performance, video, food art, participatory practice, and beyond); research-based and interdisciplinary practices are especially encouraged. The Centre works at the intersection of art, science, and (agro)ecological practices, with a focus on multispecies coexistence and climate change in the Mediterranean, and has been based in Palermo since 2021. PROGRAMME: five phases (orientation with small trips and gatherings; observation with independent fieldwork and consultations with artists, curators, scientists, and anthropologists; proposal and ecological feasibility developed with mentors; creation; and public restitution with an open event, a workshop, and a public discussion). Each artist develops an independent site-specific intervention; the two residents share the site, living space, and process, with exchange encouraged but not prescribed. Artist duos and artists who want to collaborate are encouraged to apply (you can apply as an individual for one position, as a duo for both, or as two artists collaborating for two positions). Mentorship by co-founders/artistic directors Fabio Aranzulla and Luca Cinquemani (weekly site visits, studio discussions, work-in-progress presentations); logistics managed by Francesca Farris. WHO: artists whose work engages with ecology, multispecies relations, or the more-than-human, who can move between observation and making and are interested in dialogue with scientists, land workers, and local communities. Priority is given to artists from working-class and less-privileged backgrounds, those who self-identify as part of marginalised communities, and candidates reflecting diversity in gender, age, geographical background, and ability; green travel is prioritised as a condition of participation where feasible. Applicants should be open to living and working in a neighbourhood on the margins of Palermo's periphery. Funded by Culture Moves Europe (Creative Europe programme of the European Union). APPLICATION: first phase via the online form (opened 10 June); shortlisted artists contacted before the end of July; second phase is video calls with shortlisted candidates; applications accepted in English and Italian. Contact: contact.cmrfe@gmail.com. Deadline 5 July 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: AY-ROOP / Le Milieu: Reuse - (Re)making for Circus Residency 2026 (Rennes Métropole, France)
item.description:
Organisation: AY-ROOP

Location: Le Milieu, Saint-Jacques-de-la-Lande, Rennes Métropole, France. Applicants must legally reside in a Creative Europe country other than France and the French overseas territories (DROM-COM).

Award: Funded through Culture Moves Europe: EUR 900 mobility allowance per person plus flat-rate travel coverage; supplements for green mobility, visa fees, dependent children under 18, OCT/OR residents, and disability/accessibility needs (including possible full coverage of a sign-language interpreter). On-site single-room accommodation, lunches at Le Milieu canteen (Tuesday to Friday), shared kitchen, and technical support provided. No application fee.

Duration: 21-day residency, 28 November to 18 December 2026.

Deadline: Closed 03 Jul 2026

AY-ROOP is launching an open call to select three circus artists and/or circus set designers/builders for a 21-day Culture Moves Europe research residency at Le Milieu (Saint-Jacques-de-la-Lande, Rennes Métropole) from 28 November to 18 December 2026. The residency is a research laboratory on reuse in contemporary circus: how everyday, locally available materials (paper/cardboard, plastics, lightweight metal, textiles, reclaimed wood, bicycle parts, etc.) can be used to create scenographic elements, sets, props and small apparatuses for juggling, acrobatics and balancing. The core principle is that objects are recreated locally from resources available in each territory rather than transported. AY-ROOP provides an equipped construction workshop, a rehearsal stage and circus tent, a guide to local reuse-sector contacts, and a full residency environment (accommodation, shared kitchen, working facilities); two mentors (a reuse specialist and a performing-arts professional) support the artists. PROFILES: circus artists interested in construction/scenography/DIY making, and builders/set designers working in circus; individual or group applications (max 3 people per group); emerging or established, any background. Accessible to artists with disabilities and accessibility needs. Working language English. No aerial apparatus or rigging: all work is ground-based. ELIGIBILITY: aged 18+ (no upper limit); legally resident in a Creative Europe country other than France and DROM-COM; available for the full residency. APPLY with a single PDF (1-page bio/CV, 5-page portfolio or video links, 1-page statement of intent, proof of residence). DEADLINE: 3 July 2026. Results announced 13 July 2026. Check the linked call PDF for full details.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Visual Anthropology Center (VAC): Residency Programme (Belgrade)
item.description:
Organisation: Visual Anthropology Center (VAC)

Location: Belgrade, Serbia (accommodation and studio in central Belgrade; additional workspace at Community Center KROV)

Award: EUR 1,000–1,500 per month (stipend, varies by project requirements), plus accommodation and studio space provided. No application fee.

Duration: Approximately 1–2 months; flexible packages depending on project needs

Deadline: 31 Dec 2027

Visual Anthropology Center (VAC) in Belgrade invites filmmakers, visual anthropologists, artists, and researchers to apply for its residency programme. The residency specialises in visual anthropology but is also open to individual and collaborative projects in experimental ethnography, creative documentary film, and other artistic mediums. Residents may apply with a project aimed at a public showing, or use the time and space to reflect, research, or write without a specific outcome. WHAT IS PROVIDED: accommodation and studio space in central Belgrade; additional workspace and community connection through Community Center KROV (an independent creative hub hosting lectures, events, and exhibitions); project guidance and mentorship from film professionals, ethnomusicologists, producers, artists, curators, and anthropologists; connections to local cultural and academic institutions, archives, galleries, and exhibition spaces; access to camera equipment, translators for fieldwork or editing, fixers, and project-specific services or materials; opportunity to exhibit a final project as an exhibition, installation, screening, workshop, or lecture. Eligible projects may be promoted through VAC's platforms and partner networks, including the Vizantrop Film Festival, for regional and international visibility. ELIGIBILITY: filmmakers, visual anthropologists, artists, and researchers; individual and collaborative projects welcome. Applications accepted on a rolling basis.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Simone Veil Prize for European Jewish Cultural Heritage 2026
item.description:
Organisation: European Commission, Directorate-General for Education, Youth, Sport and Culture

Location: EU Member States and third countries associated to the Creative Europe programme

Award: €10,000 per Grand Prix winner (five winners, one per category); up to 25 finalists recognized. No application fee.

Duration: For projects completed between January 2023 and January 2026

Deadline: 31 Jul 2026

The Simone Veil Prize, administered by the European Commission, recognises outstanding projects preserving and promoting European Jewish cultural heritage. It is awarded across five categories: Shared Histories, Heritage for Dialogue, Living Heritage, Cross-border Cooperation and Shared Narratives, and Next Generation/Youth Engagement and Education. AWARD: €10,000 per Grand Prix winner (one per category, five in total); up to 25 finalists receive recognition. ELIGIBILITY: legal or natural persons (individuals or organisations) registered in EU Member States or third countries associated to the Creative Europe programme; projects must have been completed between January 2023 and January 2026. DEADLINE: 31 July 2026 at 23:59 CET. APPLY by submitting the completed application form by email to applications@simoneveilprize.eu.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: MoTA Museum of Transitory Art: Production Residency 2027 (Ljubljana)
item.description:
Organisation: MoTA - Museum of Transitory Art

Location: Ljubljana, Slovenia. Open internationally to artists of all nationalities and professional levels.

Award: Full support including accommodation, technical support, production costs and an artist fee. The exact EUR artist-fee amount is not published; confirm by email. No application fee.

Duration: Production residency of up to one month.

Deadline: 15 Sep 2026

MoTA (Museum of Transitory Art) in Ljubljana runs an annual open call for production residencies for media, sound and visual artists, curators and researchers. Production residencies offer full support including accommodation, technical support, production costs and an artist fee; the exact artist-fee amount is not published on the official or aggregator pages, so applicants should confirm the figure directly by email. Residencies run up to one month. Open internationally: artists of all nationalities and at all professional levels are encouraged to apply, as individuals or collectives. No application fee. The annual deadline is 15 September (for residencies the following year). Apply by email to mota.artists@gmail.com with the subject line Application for production residency. Note: the official motamuseum.com SSL certificate is currently expired, which may briefly affect link reliability.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: FUTURES Photography: 2027 Residency Open Call
item.description:
Organisation: FUTURES Photography

Location: Futures Hub, Amsterdam, Netherlands; ISSP, Riga, Latvia; Capa Center, Budapest, Hungary

Award: EUR 1,000 stipend; accommodation provided. No application fee.

Duration: Minimum 1 month; location-specific dates in 2027 (Amsterdam: Jan, Feb, Apr, May, Jun, Sep, Oct, Nov, Dec; Riga: May; Budapest: Apr)

Deadline: Closed 22 Jun 2026

ELIGIBILITY: Open exclusively to current FUTURES artists (existing members of the FUTURES Photography platform); the application is free of charge. CONCEPT: The residency supports emerging photographers with time, space, and resources to develop new work, with a strong emphasis on research and artistic process. Artists are invited to explore the intersection of Nature, Environment, and Technology - projects should challenge perceptions of human and ecological relationships, technological advancement, and environmental responsibility. An interdisciplinary approach is encouraged, and each residency culminates in a public event where artists share their work and engage with diverse audiences. LOCATIONS: Three partner venues host residencies at different times in 2027. (1) Futures Hub, Amsterdam (global FUTURES HQ): a 60 sqm ground-floor studio with heating, electricity, and private entrance; live-work space with double folding bed, private bathroom, washing machine, and small kitchen; sliding door separates work and living areas; access to a shared atrium for large-scale works, performances, and presentations; communal rooftop terrace; bed linens and towels provided; available January, February, April, May, June, September, October, November, December 2027. (2) ISSP, Riga: private bedroom with working space in a shared city-centre apartment with two other artists; spacious shared living room and fully equipped kitchen; access to ISSP office facilities including printer, scanner, ISSP photography library, and studio lights; available May 2027. (3) Capa Center, Budapest: fully furnished private room in a communal apartment at Art Quarter Budapest; 400 sqm shared studio in a postindustrial area; services to integrate residents into the local Budapest art scene and establish professional connections; option to organise a presentation at Capa Centre in downtown Budapest; access to Capa Centre office facilities (printer, scanner); available April 2027. PRESENCE: minimum 90% presence required throughout the residency period. ENGAGEMENT: residents contribute to a Visual Diary (one Instagram carousel and two Stories, in collaboration with FUTURES), a short Video Portrait documenting their process, a public event such as an open studio or workshop (open studios on the last Wednesday of the month), and a written interview with a local writer published on the FUTURES website. FINANCIAL SUPPORT: EUR 1,000 stipend to cover living expenses, materials, and travel; accommodation is provided at all locations. Residents are responsible for arranging travel documents, health and travel insurance, liability insurance, and any visa requirements (support provided where needed). APPLY: via the FUTURES website; application is free and open only to FUTURES artists. TIMELINE: deadline 22 June 2026 at 23:59 Amsterdam time; results announced July 2026. Contact info@futures-photography.com for questions.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Genius Loci Weimar: Video Mapping Competition 2026 (Democracy and Society)
item.description:
Organisation: Genius Loci Weimar

Location: Weimar, Germany. Open to international artists; winning works are projected onto historic facades during the festival.

Award: EUR 7,000 production budget per winning facade (one winner selected per building) plus a EUR 3,000 festival prize for one selected work. No application fee.

Duration: Festival 11-13 September 2026; deliverable is a roughly 5-minute projection-mapping work for a facade.

Deadline: Closed 14 Jun 2026

Genius Loci Weimar is an annual projection-mapping (video mapping) festival that invites artists to design audiovisual works for the facades of historic Weimar buildings. The 2026 competition theme is Democracy and Society. Entry is at concept stage: submissions are conceptual proposals, and professional video-mapping expertise is not required, as the organisers provide technical support to realise winning concepts. One winner is selected per facade and receives a EUR 7,000 production budget; one selected work additionally receives a EUR 3,000 festival prize. Open to international artists working as individuals or in teams. Winners are announced around 6 July 2026 and the festival runs 11-13 September 2026. Application deadline: 14 June 2026, 23:59 CET. Register and submit via the official competition page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: CreArt 3.0: Residency at Chalet Lecoq 2026 (Clermont-Ferrand)
item.description:
Organisation: City of Clermont-Ferrand (CreArt network)

Location: Chalet Lecoq, Jardin Lecoq, Clermont-Ferrand, France.

Award: Residency grant EUR 3,000 per artist for the two-month residency (EUR 1,500/month), plus EUR 600 production costs per artist. For European (CreArt network) artists: reimbursement of round-trip travel up to EUR 500, and accommodation (a two-bedroom apartment) plus a fully-equipped workspace; the Clermont-Ferrand artist receives the workspace. No application fee.

Duration: Two-month residency, 26 October to 20 December 2026

Deadline: Closed 21 Jun 2026

As part of the CreArt network, the City of Clermont-Ferrand in France is organising a two-month residency for three visual artists at Chalet Lecoq, selecting two artists from the CreArt network and one from Clermont-Ferrand. Both central and atypical, set in the middle of a public garden (the Jardin Lecoq, a park in the heart of the city), the Chalet Lecoq is the former janitor's house of the park and invites reflection on the relationship between nature and the urban, in a place frequented by residents of all ages. It offers a privileged setting with a two-bedroom apartment and a fully-equipped workspace, and welcomes international artists in residency throughout the year. An end-of-residency presentation may be organised in discussion with the hosted artists. CONDITIONS: residency grant of EUR 3,000 per artist (EUR 1,500/month); EUR 600 production costs per artist; for European artists, reimbursement of round-trip travel up to EUR 500; accommodation and workspace for the two European artists, and workspace for the Clermont-Ferrand artist. ELIGIBILITY: artists born or resident in one of the following CreArt cities (Kaunas, Lithuania; Liepaja, Latvia; Skopje, North Macedonia; Aveiro, Portugal; Valladolid, Spain; Lublin, Poland; Venice, Italy; Rouen, France; Ceske Budejovice, Czech Republic; Oulu, Finland; Regensburg, Germany); members of the Croatian Association of Visual Artists (HDLU); and Ukrainian artists through cooperation with the Lviv Art Council 'Dialog'. APPLY in English (a single PDF with ID/passport and residency certificate if required, a one-page CV, a portfolio of max 10 pages/10 images, and a one-page project proposal) via the CreArt artists' platform. A jury of Clermont-Ferrand contemporary art professionals selects the artists, with particular attention to work that may reveal forms of connection. Deadline: 21 June 2026 (midnight); selected candidates announced 3 July 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: SVĚTOVA 1: As We Grow V (Educational & Research Program) 2026
item.description:
Organisation: SVĚTOVA 1

Location: Prague, Czech Republic; participants must be physically present in Prague for the entire program.

Award: Scholarship of CZK 5,000 (around EUR 200). Free 14-day educational programme; work exhibited at SVĚTOVA 1 for at least six weeks with production and PR support. NO accommodation provided (funding restrictions). No application fee.

Duration: 14 days, 7 to 20 September 2026

Deadline: Closed 30 Jun 2026

SVĚTOVA 1 invites early-career artists and cultural organizers to apply for the fifth edition of its educational and research programme As We Grow. This year the course is led by artist and mythopoet András Cséfalvay, Associate Professor at the Academy of Fine Arts in Bratislava. The 14-day project (7 to 20 September 2026) culminates in a group exhibition. WHAT YOU GET: a free 14-day educational experience; a scholarship of CZK 5,000 (around EUR 200); a rich working environment with on-site interaction with the leading artist, scholars and other artists; active involvement in the As We Grow research and event programming; organizational and professional support; and exhibition of your work at SVĚTOVA 1 for at least six weeks, with production and PR support before, during and after the show. SVĚTOVA 1 actively encourages queer and BIPoC people who have migrated to or taken refuge in Prague to apply. ELIGIBILITY: applicants must be physically present in Prague for the entire program, have essential spoken English, and commit to the whole 14 days after acceptance. Note: accommodation cannot be offered due to funding restrictions. APPLY via the online Notion form with a portfolio of 3 works, a CV (max 2 pages), and either a 2-3 minute video or a 250-word motivation letter (may include images) outlining how your lived experience and interest align with the course. Deadline: 30 June 2026 at midnight; results announced by 15 July 2026. Questions: tomas@svetova1.cz.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: TRACE: Artistic Residencies on Memory, Displacement & Contemporary Europe 2026-27
item.description:
Organisation: Camera a Sud (Italy), Forgotten Children of War Association (Bosnia and Herzegovina) and The 4th Block (Ukraine)

Location: Italy, Bosnia and Herzegovina, and online (Ukraine); open to artists based in Creative Europe programme countries.

Award: NO daily artist fee. Travel, accommodation and meals covered during programme activities (up to the maximum allowed under Creative Europe regulations). Technical equipment for producing artworks is NOT provided. Plus international visibility/PR, exhibitions, mentorship and a certificate. No application fee.

Duration: Research missions in 2026 (Bosnia and Herzegovina Jul/Aug, Italy Oct, Ukraine online); residencies in 2027 for 6 selected artists

Deadline: Closed 18 Jun 2026

TRACE is an international artistic residency programme, funded by the EU's Creative Europe programme, exploring how contemporary art can engage with memory, war, displacement, ecological crisis and the collective experiences shaping Europe today. It takes as starting points recent and ongoing crises, including the conflict in Bosnia and Herzegovina and the Srebrenica genocide, Mediterranean migration, the ecological legacy of Chernobyl, and the Russian invasion of Ukraine, bringing together artists, curators, researchers and local communities to work with archives, sites of memory and present-day social realities. WHO THEY SEEK: artists whose practice engages themes such as memory and remembrance, identity, war and its consequences, migration and displacement, archives and documentary practices, the relationship between past and present, and ecological and social transformation, working across visual arts, photography, film and video, sound art, illustration, graphic storytelling, podcasts and audio, or interdisciplinary practice. No age restrictions. ELIGIBILITY: artists based in countries participating in Creative Europe (EU member states; Iceland, Norway, Liechtenstein; and accession/candidate/potential-candidate countries including Albania, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Georgia, Kosovo, Montenegro, North Macedonia, Serbia, Ukraine, Armenia and Tunisia). STRUCTURE: an international jury selects 15 participants for three research and workshop programmes (Bosnia and Herzegovina Jul/Aug 2026, Italy Oct 2026, Ukraine online); 6 artists are then selected to continue into the 2027 residency programme (on-site in Italy and Bosnia and Herzegovina, plus an online residency connected to Ukraine with The 4th Block), with outcomes shown in international exhibitions in the partner countries (the Ukrainian exhibition in a safe location in Lviv) and the TRACE digital archive. WHAT PARTICIPANTS RECEIVE: coverage of travel, accommodation and meals during programme activities (up to Creative Europe limits), international visibility and PR support, access to archives and communities, mentorship, professional documentation and a certificate; there is NO daily artist fee and no production equipment. English is the working language. APPLY via the Google Form with a portfolio (max 5MB PDF) or website, a short project proposal/research idea (max 1 page) and a short biography. Deadline: 18 June 2026 (Rome time, UTC+2); selected participants contacted by 28 June. Questions: info@tracearts.eu.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Platform: Residency Program 2027 (Loosey-Goosey), Vaasa, Finland
item.description:
Organisation: Platform

Location: Vaasa, Finland; artist must spend at least 80 percent of the residency in Vaasa.

Award: Fully funded. Working grant of EUR 42/day (proportional to days spent in Vaasa), a material budget up to EUR 1,000 (reimbursed against original receipts for project-related purchases), and travel covered (one return trip, cheapest option). Accommodation provided. No application fee.

Duration: 4 to 12 weeks during 2027 (applicant specifies preferred months)

Deadline: Closed 30 Jun 2026

Platform is an art organization in Vaasa, a relatively peripheral Finnish town, running an art space and a residency. The 2027 theme, Loosey-Goosey, invites working artists to explore the limits of their expression and question professionalism: what happens to our art when we define and limit it to conform to society's lines, and who decides what makes a professional artist. Platform seeks artists who work with the topic of professionalism, questioning it or rejecting result-driven societal pressures and norms. The residency is open to artists of any medium, including but not limited to installation, performance, sound art, textiles, sculpture, video, community art, and painting. SUPPORT: a fully funded residency with a private apartment (bedroom and kitchen) within walking distance of the dedicated studio and project space; a working grant of EUR 42/day (proportional to days spent in Vaasa); a material budget up to EUR 1,000 reimbursed against receipts; and one return trip (cheapest option) covered. A contact person helps the artist settle in, source materials and make connections. Platform holds basic tools and electronics (projectors, Zoom recorder, office printer, sound system, sewing machine, etc.). DURATION: 4 to 12 weeks during 2027; artists who can stay up to 12 weeks are preferred, though 4-8 week requests are considered, and duos may apply. EXPECTATIONS: give a public talk, performance, workshop or presentation at the start; communicate in English, Swedish or Finnish; spend at least 80 percent of the residency in Vaasa; and submit project documentation plus a short report for the website. Platform does not run a gallery, so no end-of-residency exhibition is offered, but it will help arrange a final presentation, studio visit or performance if wanted. APPLY: via the online form only, with a single PDF (max 10 pages, 5 MB) named FIRSTNAME_LASTNAME containing (1) an informal one-page project plan and motivation letter stating preferred 2027 period, (2) descriptions and images of 5 previous projects or artworks (direct video links for videos), and (3) a CV of max two pages including name, date of birth and current location. Open call: 8 June to 30 June 2026, 23:59 EEST. All applicants notified by the end of September 2026; the board makes the selection and is not obliged to justify it.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: ARCAthens: Athens Residency Fellowships (Fall 2026)
item.description:
Organisation: ARCAthens

Location: Athens, Greece (in-person residency). Open to applicants not residing in Greece

Award: Fully funded: USD 3,000 Fellowship Prize, USD 200/week stipend toward food and local transport, basic coach round-trip airfare reimbursed, private bedroom and bathroom with shared kitchen, and studio access for Visual Art Fellows. No application fee.

Duration: 3 months, 10 September to 30 November 2026

Deadline: Closed 30 Jun 2026

The Athens Residency is the flagship program of ARCAthens, a not-for-profit founded in 2017, offering two fully-funded Fellowships (Visual Art and Curatorial) that enable artists, curators, and scholars to live and work in Athens, Greece, and respond to the city's ongoing cultural renaissance. The residency is a structured, research-driven program built around scheduled engagements with artists, curators, institutions, and collections, including arranged studio and private collection visits, guided institutional access, and select community events. Distinct from studio-isolated models, it emphasizes engagement and exchange, balancing focused visits with time for independent exploration. ELIGIBILITY: ARCAthens welcomes applicants from all countries and nationalities representing a broad range of creative thought and practice; the program is open to applicants not residing in Greece. PRACTICAL: 3-month duration; USD 3,000 Fellowship Prize; USD 200 per week stipend toward food and local transportation; a basic coach round-trip ticket is provided (Fellows purchase following ARCAthens approval of itinerary and cost, reimbursed within five business days; in cases of financial hardship ARCAthens may arrange travel directly). Fellows are responsible for all other expenses, including food, local transportation, and any production, installation, or shipping costs. Visas are not necessary for US nationals for the duration of the program; others are responsible for procuring visas as applicable. Facilities include a private bedroom and bathroom with shared kitchen; Visual Art Fellows have studio access. Two Fellows are in residence at one time. Working language is English. Selection is by a 3-person independent committee of arts professionals. No fee, open call. TIMELINE: application deadline 30 June 2026 (closes 11:00 p.m. ET); notification of Fellowships mid July; residency 10 September to 30 November 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: CIRCUS LOOPS: European Residency Open Call 2026-2028
item.description:
Organisation: Circusstad Festival (NL), Zirkus ON (DE) and Cirkus Kolektiv (HR), co-funded by Creative Europe

Location: Residencies in Split (Croatia), Karlsruhe (Germany) and Rotterdam (Netherlands)

Award: Each selected core team member receives a fixed grant of EUR 3,746; travel, accommodation and per diems are covered by the project partners. No application fee.

Duration: Three 10-day residencies between November 2026 and July 2027; final presentations at partner festivals 2027-2028

Deadline: Closed 17 Jun 2026

CIRCUS LOOPS is a European cooperation project (2026-2028) led by Circusstad Festival (NL), Zirkus ON (DE) and Cirkus Kolektiv (HR), co-funded by the Creative Europe Programme, inviting contemporary circus artists and collectives to a residency programme dedicated to developing new work through audience engagement. It centres on the Feedback Loops methodology, integrating audience perspectives into the artistic creation process. Selected projects take part in three 10-day international residencies in Split, Karlsruhe and Rotterdam between November 2026 and July 2027 (all projects in residence simultaneously in each location, working in separate spaces), presenting work-in-progress and engaging in facilitated feedback sessions with audiences, peers and experts. The programme concludes with final presentations at partner festivals between 2027 and 2028. ELIGIBILITY: contemporary circus artists and small companies based in Europe (including Creative Europe countries) developing a project at an early or mid stage of creation; applicants must be available for all residency periods and commit to the full programme; teams limited to three core members. SUPPORT: a fixed grant of EUR 3,746 per core team member, plus travel, accommodation and per diems covered. APPLY via the official online form (project description, artistic team info, motivation and video material), in English, by 17 June 2026, 23:59 CET. An international jury selects three projects on artistic quality, motivation, feasibility and relevance to the Feedback Loops methodology; results published by 25 July 2026. Contact: circusloops@gmail.com.

Open call details

item.title: Museum Dhondt-Dhaenens: MDD Collective Open Call 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Museum Dhondt-Dhaenens (MDD) youth collective

Location: Museum Dhondt-Dhaenens garden, Sint-Martens-Latem, Belgium (outdoor presentation)

Award: EUR 500 material budget plus a EUR 350 participation fee per selected artist; transport and insurance of the works are fully covered by the organisation. No application fee.

Duration: One-weekend presentation (11-12 July 2026)

Deadline: Closed 14 Jun 2026

Open call from Museum Dhondt-Dhaenens' youth collective inviting three to four emerging artists to present a work in the museum garden over the weekend of 11-12 July 2026, in dialogue with its surroundings, architecture and landscape. The theme centres on the idea of the 'frame', starting from Isa Genzken's work Fenster in the museum garden, exploring how a frame determines what we see and what stays just outside our field of vision. ELIGIBILITY: emerging artists aged 31 or under interested in joining MDD's youth project. Apply via the Museum Dhondt-Dhaenens open-call page; questions to opencall@museumdd.be.

item.title: ifa: Rave Scholarship - Cultural Heritage and Museum Transformation (2027)
item.description:
Organisation: ifa (Institut fuer Auslandsbeziehungen) - Alexander Rave Foundation

Location: Host institutions in Germany (applicants must be cultural practitioners from countries on the OECD DAC list)

Award: Net payment of EUR 1,500 per month; monthly family allowances for accompanying family (EUR 250 spouse, EUR 250 per child); visa costs; outward and return travel costs; health insurance (including family); monthly public transport pass for the city of residence; up to EUR 500 for a language course. No application fee.

Duration: 3 to 6 months at a German host institution (with optional supplementary 2-12 week tandem stay in the DAC-list country if synergies arise; both stays in the same year)

Deadline: Closed 30 Jun 2026

Scholarship from ifa (Institut fuer Auslandsbeziehungen), supported by the Alexander Rave Foundation, on the management of cultural heritage and the transformation of museums and exhibition centres. It enables cultural practitioners from countries on the OECD DAC list to stay with host institutions in order to deepen their professional knowledge transnationally, introduce new perspectives and build long-term cooperation through exchange. The focus is on transcultural dialogue and engaging with cultural heritage, collections and archive records, funding mutual knowledge transfer and the creation of sustainable networks, and supporting freedom in art and science, freedom of opinion and open debates. Thematic focal areas centre on transforming museums and exhibition venues in line with the ICOM definition of museums (2023): cultural participation, sustainability, non-discriminatory spaces and decolonial working methods. ELIGIBILITY: curators, restorers, mediators and culture managers from DAC-list countries. Scholarships begin after 1 February 2027.

Open call details

item.title: IMMA Dwell Here: One Year Residency Open Call 2027
item.description:
Organisation: Irish Museum of Modern Art (IMMA)

Location: Dublin, Ireland (onsite at IMMA)

Award: Funded. Monthly EUR 1,000 bursary for pre-agreed contracted months; free onsite accommodation (separate to studio); access to an onsite IMMA studio (if required); amenities (electricity, heating, Wi-Fi, parking); one return journey for residents living outside Ireland; pre-agreed material/programming expenses related to the Research Assemblies. NOTE: EUR 10 application fee via Submittable.

Duration: Up to 11 months between January and December 2027 (start no earlier than 18 January 2027; all residencies finished by 15 December 2027)

Deadline: Closed 22 Jun 2026

IMMA's Dwell Here One Year Residency offers research-led residencies in Dublin across the visual arts, design, architecture, curation and related humanities fields, supporting independent site-responsive research. Structured around three week-long seasonal Research Assemblies in 2027 (Spring 03-09 March, Summer 09-15 June, Autumn 22-28 September) where residents engage with mentors, peers, fellow residents and the wider IMMA community through workshops, temporary exhibitions, reading groups, walking tours, open studios, talks, site visits, performances or other studio-related activities. RESEARCH THEMES (encouraged but not required): Technologies of Peace (commemorative landscapes, memories of peace, sustainable coexistence); The Irish Paradigm (Ireland as small island on the edge of Europe, periphery vs centre); The Museum as a Site of Vibration (museum and site within the built legacy of empire, erased/censored/marginalised histories, planetary care, hospitality). ELIGIBILITY: artists and thinkers based in Ireland or internationally, committed to research excellence within their discipline; framed around research excellence rather than career stage. Some onsite accommodation suitable for partners or small families (discuss with the residency programmer); self-catering facility. Notification September 2026. APPLY via Submittable (EUR 10 fee). NOTE: there are two other Dwell Here open calls for 2027 (additional one-year and one-month residencies); applicants can only apply for ONE Dwell Here residency.

Open call details

item.title: Kunstverein Reutlingen: Open Call - 800 m2 Open Space 2027
item.description:
Organisation: Kunstverein Reutlingen (Reutlingen Art Association)

Location: Wandel-Hallen, Eberhardstr. 14, Reutlingen, Germany

Award: EUR 5,000 production budget per artist or group, allocated by the artist across production costs, transport and artist fees. The Kunstverein additionally covers advertising/PR, catering at the opening and closing events, supervision, on-site insurance from delivery until 31 July 2027, and photographic documentation; a volunteer team assists with setup and dismantling. Additional third-party funding may be pursued after the concept is selected. No application fee.

Duration: Exhibition 14 March - 18 July 2027 (dismantling 19-31 July)

Deadline: Closed 15 Jun 2026

Open call from Kunstverein Reutlingen inviting artists or groups of artists to develop a site-specific concept for the entire 800 m2 of the Wandel-Hallen (1st floor) in Reutlingen, Germany, for a 2027 solo-presentation slot. The concept may use any artistic form (painting, sculpture, installation, photography, performance, etc.) and may include existing works as well as new productions. ELIGIBILITY: all participants must currently have a residence and/or studio in Germany; applicants must have artistic training or extensive and recognised exhibition activity, or be a student at a German state art academy or recognised independent art college in fine arts. One concept per artist or group. APPLY: register on the Kunstverein portal between 1 April and 15 June 2026 with contact details, an artistic CV, three comparative images of previous work, and a PDF (max 2 MB) artistic concept including a project description with exact dimensions, meaningful images/models, and a detailed financing plan allocating the EUR 5,000 budget across production, transport and fees. Selection by an expert jury (the Kunstverein director and two external jurors); results communicated by end of June 2026 at the earliest. Existing on-site technology includes two projectors, a cinema screen, four LED theater spotlights and a lighting console, two Bluetooth speakers, four Bluetooth-capable studio monitors and two wireless headphones. Contact: info@kunstverein-reutlingen.de.

Open call details

item.title: Kunstler*innen Vereinigung Tirol: Annual Programme 2027 - Thresholds (Kunstpavillon & Neue Galerie)
item.description:
Organisation: Kunstler*innen Vereinigung Tirol

Location: Kunstpavillon and Neue Galerie, Innsbruck, Austria

Award: Maximum EUR 3,500 production budget for a Kunstpavillon project or EUR 3,000 for a Neue Galerie project (covers production, display/architecture, technical rentals, materials, travel, accommodation and transport), plus a separate artist fee of up to EUR 2,000 (incl. VAT) and an additional EUR 250 fee for an accompanying artist talk. The institution additionally covers printing, translation, set-up team, marketing, PR and insurance. No application fee.

Duration: One exhibition slot within the 2027 annual programme (January 2027 - January 2028, average 9-13 weeks per exhibition)

Deadline: Closed 30 Jun 2026

Open call for the 2027 annual programme of Kunstler*innen Vereinigung Tirol at the Kunstpavillon (approx. 175 m2 skylight gallery) and the Neue Galerie (95 m2 vaulted space, easy to darken for projections), Innsbruck. The 2027 theme 'Thresholds' invites projects that highlight counter-models or counter-cultural dynamics to conservative, anti-modern and authoritarian backlash, exploring keywords like democracy, complexity, flash crash, anti-modern tendencies and misogyny. Up to five exhibition projects are selected. ELIGIBILITY: international call open to artists from all artistic disciplines, media and practices; concrete projects, concepts, artistic interventions and portfolios accepted. Selection is made regardless of nationality, ethnic or social background, age, disability, sexual identity or gender. APPLY (German or English) via the online platform with a completed data sheet, preferred venue, a max 500-word concept (plus a 1,200-character short description), visuals, technical info, a portfolio (films via Vimeo/YouTube/Dropbox; no WeTransfer), CVs, and a cost calculation; one PDF up to 10 MB. Postal submissions also possible (Kunstler*innen Vereinigung Tirol, Kunstpavillon, Rennweg 8a, 6020 Innsbruck, Austria). Curatorial/technical production support and a FREIRAD radio conversation accompany each exhibition. Deadline 30 June 2026; selected projects contacted by autumn 2026. Contact: office@kuveti.at.

Open call details

item.title: Between Bridges: Residency January-June 2027 (Berlin)
item.description:
Organisation: Between Bridges (Wolfgang Tillmans Foundation)

Location: Keithstrasse 15, Berlin-Schoneberg, Germany

Award: Monthly stipend of EUR 1,500 (regardless of the number of participants) plus a EUR 5,000 budget for the exhibition or public presentation, and use of the Berlin premises (two work/exhibition rooms totalling 100 m2, storage, office, kitchen, bathroom). Studio visits within the Between Bridges network. Promotion via the foundation's channels. No application fee.

Duration: Six-month residency, January-June 2027, including a one-month exhibition or public presentation

Deadline: Closed 21 Jun 2026

The Between Bridges foundation, established by Wolfgang Tillmans, announces its ninth artist residency: a six-month working period (January-June 2027) at Keithstrasse 15 in Berlin-Schoneberg, including a one-month exhibition or public presentation. Residents receive a EUR 1,500 monthly stipend (regardless of the number of participants), a EUR 5,000 exhibition/presentation budget, use of the premises and studio visits within the Between Bridges network. The foundation cannot directly support exhibition installation but will help find service providers; documentation and promotion via the foundation's channels. ELIGIBILITY: professional artists, artist duos, or small groups working collaboratively. Artists enrolled at a university at the time of application are not eligible, EXCEPT those enrolled in PhD programmes. Selection is based on the quality of the application and work samples. APPLY (German or English) via the online form with a single PDF (max 10 MB, max 10 pages total) including CV, portfolio, and a short project proposal with exhibition/presentation concept (max 250 words or half an A4 page); links to video/audio works allowed. Deadline Sunday 21 June 2026, 23:59 CET; selected applicants notified in August 2026 (no individual feedback). Premises are not wheelchair-accessible and not suitable for residential use. Contact: residency@betweenbridges.net.

Open call details

item.title: Campo XS: CAMPO APERTO 2nd Edition (2026-2028 site-specific biennial, Genoa)
item.description:
Organisation: Campo XS (Carina Negrone, Martina Montagna; with Studio Sirotti)

Location: Campo XS, Piazza del Campo 40R, Genoa, Italy (historic Jewish ghetto)

Award: EUR 2,500 grant per selected project (covers transport, lodging, production/materials, printing, dismantling and any shipping), paid in two tranches (EUR 1,500 thirty days before opening; EUR 1,000 after dismantling) directly to the artists/curators by bank transfer, tax-free. Artists and curators based outside Italy receive an additional EUR 500 travel reimbursement. Free to apply.

Duration: Eight 60-day exhibition slots across 2026/27 (1 Oct 2026 - 25 Jul 2027) and 2027/28 (7 Oct 2027 - 30 Jul 2028)

Deadline: Closed 30 Jun 2026

Second edition of CAMPO APERTO, a site-specific biennial exhibition project at Campo XS in Genoa, a small (33 sqm) transparent-cube space in the historic Jewish ghetto, transformed from a former neighbourhood butcher shop. The programme selects 8 site-specific projects (60-day exhibition slots each) across two years as a laboratory for artistic and social practices engaging the surrounding neighbourhood, with exhibitions, performances and talks. The project also collaborates with the Diocesan Council for Minors and Families to bring non-typical art audiences into contact with the programme. ELIGIBILITY: national and international artists and curators, no age restrictions; projects must be led by an artist or collective in collaboration with a curator (self-curating must be justified); preference for projects pairing a national artist with an international curator (or vice versa), exploring the local urban context, with socio-ecological research, or dynamic interventions. APPLY by email (subject '2nd Edition CAMPO APERTO') to campoxs.genova@gmail.com by 30 June 2026 with an application form, project proposal, artist CV/bio and portfolio (max 30 MB), and curator CV/bio and portfolio (max 30 MB). Selected projects announced 30 July 2026.

Open call details

item.title: CROCOSMOS: Low-Tech Projection Lab Residency 2026 (Culture Moves Europe)
item.description:
Organisation: CROCOSMOS

Location: Ramonville-Saint-Agne, near Toulouse, France

Award: Daily allowance EUR 30/day (EUR 630 total for 21 days) plus a travel allowance (EUR 400 under 5,000 km / EUR 800 from 5,000 km), with optional add-ons: EUR 400 green-travel support, EUR 200 per child for residents with children under 18, EUR 120 visa support, and accessibility support as needed. Accommodation provided by CROCOSMOS. Visa support letters available. No application fee.

Duration: 21-day residency, 14 September - 4 October 2026 (travel days not included)

Deadline: Closed 16 Jun 2026

Collaborative residency at CROCOSMOS near Toulouse, exploring analog projection and low-tech visual creation, hosted under Culture Moves Europe. Five European visual artists experiment with upcycled projectors, liquids, drawing, mapping and collective artistic research alongside three mentors and professional facilitators, combining technical learning, collaborative creation and a public sharing at the Visiophare festival. THREE PARTS: (1) seven days building five eco-projectors by converting recycled overhead projectors into LED devices and creating analog-visual-effect accessories, using FabRiquet machines (3D printers, laser cutters); (2) five days experimenting with analog visual techniques such as liquid projections, drawing and low-tech mapping; (3) six days connecting with the community via workshops with local people and Visiophare-community artists, an analog performance and the Visiophare festival. ELIGIBILITY: 18+, no upper age limit; emerging and established artists welcome; applicants must be residents of a Creative Europe eligible country or territory (61 eligible; FRANCE is NOT eligible). Selected residents may not participate in another Culture Moves Europe residency project between 2025 and 2028. Mainly in English. APPLY by 16 June 2026 (midnight France time); results by 22 June 2026. Contact: contact@crocosmos.com.

Open call details

item.description:
Organisation: Irish Museum of Modern Art (IMMA)

Location: Dublin, Ireland (locally-based; commutable to IMMA; NO accommodation provided)

Award: Funded. EUR 4,000 bursary for the year; free access to an onsite IMMA studio; amenities (electricity, heating, Wi-Fi, parking); pre-agreed material/programming expenses related to the Research Assemblies. IMMA's Dwell Here One Year Studio Residency is the locally-based variant of the Dwell Here 2027 programme, supporting artists and thinkers commutable to IMMA in Dublin to undertake independent site-responsive research from an onsite studio (without accommodation). Open across visual arts, design, architecture, curation and related humanities fields. item.title: IMMA Dwell Here: One Month Residency Open Call 2027
item.description:
Organisation: Irish Museum of Modern Art (IMMA)

Location: Dublin, Ireland (onsite at IMMA; open to worldwide applicants)

Award: Funded. EUR 250/week bursary for pre-agreed contracted weeks; free shared onsite accommodation (with own bedroom, separate to studio); access to a shared onsite IMMA studio (if required); amenities (electricity, heating, Wi-Fi, parking); one return journey for residents living outside Ireland. NOTE: EUR 10 application fee via Submittable. NOTE: cannot support accommodation for additional family members or partners.

Duration: One month (four weeks); three weeks of independent research plus one week that must overlap with a selected 2027 Research Assembly (Spring, Summer, or Autumn)

Deadline: Closed 22 Jun 2026

IMMA's Dwell Here One Month Residency is the short-format variant of the Dwell Here 2027 programme, open to nationwide and internationally based artists and thinkers to undertake site-responsive research at IMMA in Dublin across visual arts, design, architecture, curation and related humanities fields. STRUCTURE: residents undertake three weeks of independent site-responsive research plus a fourth week (beginning, middle, or end) that must overlap with one of the three 2027 Research Assemblies (Spring 03-09 March, Summer 09-15 June, or Autumn 22-28 September). During the assembly week, residents engage with mentors, peers, fellow residents and the wider IMMA community through workshops, temporary exhibitions, reading groups, walking tours, open studios, talks, site visits, performances or other studio-related activities. Intent: deeper insight into the context of visual arts practices in Ireland, strengthening international connections and seeding ideas for proposed return residencies/programming. RESEARCH THEMES (encouraged but not required): Technologies of Peace; The Irish Paradigm; The Museum as a Site of Vibration. ELIGIBILITY: artists and thinkers based worldwide, committed to research excellence within their discipline. Self-catering facility; occasional hospitality. Notification October 2026. APPLY via Submittable (EUR 10 fee). NOTE: there are two other Dwell Here open calls for 2027 (One Year Residency with accommodation, and One Year Studio Residency for local applicants without accommodation); applicants can only apply for ONE Dwell Here residency.

item.title: OASis In the Park: Second Call (Parco San Laise, Naples) 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Fondazione Campania Welfare

Location: Parco San Laise, Bagnoli, Naples, Italy (former NATO base undergoing social/cultural regeneration)

Award: EUR 1,300 artist fee, plus travel, meals, accommodation, and production costs fully covered by the project. No application fee.

Duration: Approximately two weeks per selected artist, scheduled between September and October 2026

Deadline: Closed 26 Jun 2026

Second round of OASis In the Park, Fondazione Campania Welfare's residency at Parco San Laise (a former NATO base in Bagnoli, Naples) following the first cycle (San Laise Open Lab). The OASis operates as a diffused platform across multiple spaces within the park, combining artistic creation, community engagement, and social activation. WHO: artists developing participatory, socially engaged practices with strong educational and/or community-focused components; particular encouragement for artists interested in working with children, youth, and local communities, though projects of broader social relevance to Bagnoli and community regeneration are welcome. DISCIPLINES (non-exhaustive): filmmaking and audiovisual media; photography; visual arts (sculpture, street art, graffiti, painting); textile, fashion and material practices; music and sound; AI-based artistic production; spatial design and installation art; lighting/light-based practices; interior and community-oriented design; illustration and graphic arts; multidisciplinary practice. ACTIVITIES: workshops, co-creation sessions, personal-but-community-based artistic projects, training initiatives, or other artistic interventions; focus refined during selection. SELECTION CRITERIA: artistic quality and originality; capacity for participatory and socially engaged practice; relevance to local context and community needs; clarity and concreteness of the proposal. APPLY by emailing fcw.oasis@gmail.com (subject line: 'OASis In the Park Application') with: CV (max 2 pages); portfolio (links, images, documentation); artistic project proposal (max 2 pages describing the project, community engagement plan, and intended outputs). TIMELINE: call closes 26 June 2026; artists notified July 2026; residency September-October 2026.

Open call details

CrafTopia Creative Hub, a Thessaloniki-based cultural and creative hub supporting artistic expression, learning and community engagement through residencies, workshops, exhibitions and collaborative projects, opens a Material Dialogues textile-waste residency for five visual artists. THEME: sustainability; the residency invites artists to work with textile waste sourced from industries operating in the same historic building as CrafTopia, emphasising the creative process over the final product. item.title: Aerowaves Twenty27: Call for Emerging European Choreographers
item.description:
Organisation: Aerowaves (Spring Forward festival hosted in Limerick, Ireland)

Location: Festival in Limerick, Ireland (14-17 April 2027) plus partner venues across Europe; applicants must be resident in geographical Europe

Award: For presentation at Spring Forward: a fee plus expenses including travel within Europe, hotel and a per diem/hospitality for each team member. Around 100 partner performance opportunities are guaranteed each year and supported by Aerowaves. Selected applicants additionally receive a year of Aerowaves promotion (artist profile, images, video, press coverage). No application fee.

Duration: One year of Aerowaves promotion and partner-network presentations; presentation at Spring Forward 14-17 April 2027

Deadline: 15 Jul 2026

Aerowaves selects 20 emerging choreographers based in geographical Europe each year (the Twenty27 cohort) to have their work presented at the Spring Forward festival (14-17 April 2027 in Limerick, Ireland) and at many of Aerowaves' partners around Europe; Aerowaves promotes the selected artists for a year, creating performance opportunities with partners. EVERY APPLICANT (selected or not) has the chance to be programmed by Aerowaves network partners; around 100 performance opportunities per year are supported by Aerowaves. ELIGIBILITY: applicants must be resident in geographical Europe; due to the Russian invasion of Ukraine, Aerowaves partners do NOT accept applications of works made in Russia or Belarus. ONE work per applicant per year. The work must have been produced in geographical Europe; must be 15-40 minutes; must be finished and have been presented to the public (a studio sharing counts; work-in-progress or rehearsal footage does NOT). The work should be easily included in a double or triple bill with simple technical requirements; works created for public/non-conventional spaces and outdoors are welcome. WHAT SELECTED ARTISTS GET: presentation fee at Spring Forward plus travel (within Europe), hotel and per diem/hospitality for each team member; a year of Aerowaves website promotion. DEADLINE: 15 July 2026. APPLY via the Aerowaves how-to-apply page.

Open call details

item.title: Villa Sonnenberg Artist Residency 2027 - Myths (Sep-Dec)
item.description:
Organisation: Stiftung Villa Sonnenberg

Location: Lenzburg, Aargau, Switzerland

Award: CHF 600/month per-diem (CHF 20/day) + free accommodation in private modern studio with kitchen and bathroom + use of workshop and music rehearsal room. No application or entry fee.

Duration: 3 months (mid-September to mid-December 2027); minimum 80% attendance

Deadline: Closed 07 Jun 2026

Stiftung Villa Sonnenberg's 2027 Artist-in-Residence programme on the annual theme 'Myths'. ELIGIBILITY: Professional artists working in visual arts and music. THEME: Artistic works engaging with myths in their social, personal, or political dimensions; future-oriented approaches welcome (e.g., myths in digital public spheres, gaming cultures, AI-generated visual worlds, or projects considering Villa Sonnenberg in Lenzburg and the Aargau canton). DISCIPLINES: Visual arts, music. FUNDING: Free single-use accommodation in a modern top-floor studio with private kitchen and bathroom; daily allowance of CHF 20 (CHF 600/month). Access to studio house with a 20 sqm workshop, 20 sqm music rehearsal room, and garden. APPLY: Read the 'Open Call Artist in Residence 2027' document on villasonnenberg.ch and submit CV with photo, project outline referencing the annual theme (max 2 pages), optional contribution proposal to daily life at Sonnenberg, work samples, and proof of illness/accident insurance. TIMELINE: Deadline 7 June 2026; residency mid-September to mid-December 2027. NOTE: Residents contribute through events (workshops, exhibitions, concerts) and conclude with a final public exhibition or presentation.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: FOMAS Group Special Prize 2026 (within Arte Laguna Prize)
item.description:
Organisation: FOMAS Group (in collaboration with Arte Laguna Prize)

Location: Italy (FOMAS HQ Lecco; presented within Arte Laguna at Arsenale Nord, Venice)

Award: First prize EUR 7,000; second EUR 4,000; third EUR 3,000 (all taxes included). NOTE: works are entered via the Arte Laguna Prize, which has a registration fee (approx. EUR 97-122). The winning works become FOMAS Group property (rights of economic use, reproduction, communication to the public, exhibition, distribution and digitisation transferred to FOMAS).

Duration: One-off special prize awarded within Arte Laguna Prize 2026

Deadline: Closed 01 Jun 2026

Special Prize from FOMAS Group, an Italian multinational in forging/rolling/metal powders, awarded within the Arte Laguna Prize 2026. THEME: 'the journey of steel in its crossing from hot to cold' - a metamorphosis of matter from incandescent casting to a cooled, smooth, precise surface; works should evoke this transition. FORMAT: hangable panel or canvas, dimensions between 1.5x1.5 m (minimum) and 3x2 m (maximum); unconventional materials encouraged. THREE AWARDS to nominated works: EUR 7,000 first, EUR 4,000 second, EUR 3,000 third (taxes included), paid after the work is presented. IMPORTANT CONDITION: the winning works become the property of FOMAS Group, with the artists ceding ownership and rights of economic use, reproduction, communication to the public, exhibition, distribution and digitisation for institutional and promotional purposes. APPLY by submitting via the Arte Laguna Prize portal (which has a registration fee, approx. EUR 97-122 for the full prize). Registration for the FOMAS Special Award closes 1 June 2026.

Open call details

item.title: Malakta x Kulturhuset Dux: Community Art Commission and Residency 2026 (Kristinestad)
item.description:
Organisation: Art in Malakta rf (with Kulturhuset Dux and Kristinestad Artist Residency)

Location: Kristinestad / Malax, Finland

Award: EUR 6,000 artist fee plus EUR 5,000 materials budget, with travel and residency accommodation included. No application fee.

Duration: One-month residency 1 Sep - 4 Oct 2026 (Phase 1) plus production phase, with artwork completed by summer 2027 (Phase 2)

Deadline: Closed 31 May 2026

Commission and residency from Art in Malakta rf with Kulturhuset Dux and Kristinestad Artist Residency to create a public artwork for Kulturhuset Dux or its immediate surroundings, in dialogue with Kristinestad and its residents. The medium is open (installation, object, sound, textile, light, digital); what matters is the relationship between the work, the site and the people. The project is rooted in Kristinestad's identity as a Cittaslow town and as Baltic Sea Cultural Pearl 2026. PHASE 1 (1 Sep - 4 Oct 2026): a one-month residency at Kristinestad Artist Residency for research, meetings, workshops, and artistic development. PHASE 2: production after the residency (remotely or at Malakta if needed); the artwork must be completed by summer 2027 and requires on-site presence. ELIGIBILITY: open to artists or artist collectives wanting to work site-specifically and in dialogue with residents. APPLY by email to communityart@malakta.fi by 31 May 2026 with a motivation letter, portfolio, CV and website link as a single PDF (max 5 MB). Interviews before 19 June 2026.

Open call details

item.title: FPP Residency & Exhibition 2026 (Konstkollektivet / Flower Power Photography)
item.description:
Organisation: Konstkollektivet, with Flower Power Photography, Centrum for fotografi and Artist in Coexistence (AiC)

Location: Orust and Molndal, Sweden

Award: Artist fee 7,000 SEK plus exhibition fee 3,400 SEK, travel support up to 4,000 SEK (slow travel by train/bus encouraged), and accommodation for the whole period. Plus facilitation, mentorship and research support. No application fee. (Amounts in SEK, incl. VAT where applicable.)

Duration: 14-day residency 3-17 August 2026; group exhibition opens 1 September 2026

Deadline: Closed 14 Jun 2026

Residency and exhibition for visual artists whose practice engages with experimental, alternative and/or critical image-making (analogue processes, archives or hybrid approaches), with a particular interest in image-making as a way of living and thinking alongside the planet and other beings, embracing slowness, collaboration and more-than-human care. Organised by Konstkollektivet with Flower Power Photography (an artistic research project by Sam McCarthy and Lasse Lindqvist on non-violence, ecology and more-than-human ethics), Centrum for fotografi, and Artist in Coexistence (AiC). Includes a 14-day residency on the island of Orust (3-15 August), a process presentation, a workshop contribution at Konstkollektivet (15-16 August), and participation in a group exhibition at Konstparken opening 1 September, plus accommodation, an artist fee (7,000 SEK), exhibition fee (3,400 SEK), and travel support up to 4,000 SEK. ELIGIBILITY: European-based visual artists of all ages and nationalities. APPLICATION: one PDF (max 10MB) with a project proposal/motivation (max 1 page), artist statement (max 150 words), CV (1 page) and portfolio (max 5 pages) to residens@konstkollektivet.se, subject line 'Name_LastName_FPP_Residency', by 14 June 2026.

Open call details

item.description:
Organisation: LPM - Live Performers Meeting

Location: Rome, Italy

Award: Paid open call, not a grant: participation requires an artist/guest subscription of EUR 20 (includes a pass for all activities, a T-shirt, a daily drink, and a 30-minute performance slot), plus 20% off workshops. The Live Immersive Contest offers prizes from technical partners.

Duration: Festival edition in Rome 2026

Deadline: 16 Aug 2026

Open call to participate in LPM (Live Performers Meeting), described as the biggest worldwide event dedicated to audiovisual performing arts, returning to Rome for its 27th edition. Artists can take part with AV performances, VJ sets and live mapping (a 30-minute slot to perform); workshops, lectures and project showcases; interactive AV installations; and the Live Immersive Contest (competing for prizes from technical partners). NOTE: this is a participation call rather than a funding opportunity - it requires a paid subscription (EUR 20 as artist or guest) which includes a festival pass, a 30-minute slot, a T-shirt and a daily drink, plus a 20% discount on workshops; there is also a volunteer option. Deadline to submit a proposal is 16 August 2026. Enquiries: subscriptions@liveperformersmeeting.net.

item.title: KAMP: Creative Body Institute Residency, Lesbos, Greece 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Creative Body Institute (CBI)

Location: Lesbos Island, Greece (eco-art hermitage in an olive grove on Mount Lepetymnos)

Award: Co-funded residency. Total cost per artist EUR 2,500; CBI funds EUR 1,350, so the artist contribution is EUR 1,150 (plus a EUR 100 non-refundable deposit deducted from that contribution). Does not include travel, visas, airport transfers, insurance or personal food. One full contribution waiver is available per cohort (request on the form; reviewed after selection, based on financial need and cohort balance).

Duration: Roughly 3-week residency per track; tracks run July 22 to September 26, 2026

Deadline: Closed 24 May 2026

KAMP is a communal residency from the artist-run non-profit Creative Body Institute, inviting queer-identifying artists to an ecological hermitage on Lesbos Island, Greece, to develop work exploring queer ecologies, queer temporality, and ecofeminist practices through a human and more-than-human, life-art ethos. CBI seeks artists whose practices engage embodiment, interdependence and temporality through performance, installation, sound, dance or experimental visual work. FOUR TRACKS: (1) Experimental Media and Performativity, Jul 22-Aug 10; (2) Relational and Site-Responsive Work, Aug 14-Sep 2; (3) Body-Based and Durational Practice, Sep 6-24/26; (4) Writer in Residence (dates coinciding with one of the above, for those focused on exegesis, a publication, or writing about prior embodied/artistic research). 5-7 artists are selected per cohort. Housing is simple cabin-based accommodation for relational living (Queer Family Cabin for 2-3, Cozy Cabin for solitude, Sanctuary Cabin for 2 sharing), with communal outdoor kitchen, shower house and bathrooms; this is an off-grid, sustainable environment (solar power, conscious water use, limited wifi). Artists must be comfortable with close communal living, shared rhythms, a collective food system (primarily vegetarian and pescatarian, sourced from local farmers), and unpaved terrain (which may be difficult for those with mobility impairments). INCLUDED: accommodation, shared indoor/outdoor workspaces, groceries for shared meals and staff support, two on-site CBI artist-faculty, mentorship and feedback, structured and informal group programming, work-in-progress sharing, and photo/video documentation. APPLICATION: online form (no draft saving) with project description (350 words) tied to the chosen track, 1-2 work samples, optional portfolio, artist bio(s) (350 words), and three short-answer questions; you may respond in any language. Apply individually (willing to share), as a duo, or as a trio/small group. Deadline May 24, 2026 (23:59 CET); virtual interviews May 25-29; final notifications June 1. Confirm within 5 days of acceptance with the EUR 100 deposit.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: workspacebrussels: Decoratelier Open Call 2026 (Spatial Research Residency)
item.description:
Organisation: workspacebrussels (residency at Decoratelier, Molenbeek)

Location: Decoratelier, Molenbeek, Brussels, Belgium

Award: EUR 5,000 budget (incl. VAT) for the residency, plus a 10x18m warehouse hall, wood and metal workshop access, reusable materials, junior-builder support three days a week, artistic and technical feedback, a possible internal showing, and weekday lunch. No application fee.

Duration: Three-week research residency (5 to 23 October 2026)

Deadline: Closed 31 May 2026

Research-focused residency at Decoratelier in Molenbeek, organised through workspacebrussels, for artists and collectives working with space, scenography, installation, architecture and performative environments. The residency prioritises research and material experimentation over production: applicants propose an investigation into spatial practice, ephemeral architecture or scenographic experimentation connected to the performing arts, engaging directly with the warehouse architecture and reusable materials. ELIGIBILITY: artists or collectives based in Belgium or abroad. NOTE: this is a context-specific research residency and does not provide scenographic support for existing productions. Apply via the workspacebrussels Decoratelier open call.

Open call details

item.title: Passage Art-Residency-Programme 2026 (KOBRA Cultural Network)
item.description:
Organisation: Polygona Kunstverein e.V. (KOBRA Cultural Network)

Location: Wittenberg region, Saxony-Anhalt, Germany

Award: Per residency place: accommodation for the four-week stay; a grant of EUR 5,000; artists in residence with a child receive an additional EUR 1,000 toward temporary childcare. Travel, local transport, consumables and living expenses are paid by the artist; payment is made on receipt of an invoice.

Duration: Four weeks, October 2026

Deadline: Closed 30 May 2026

Two residency places in the Passage Art-Residency-Programme run by Polygona Kunstverein e.V. as part of the KOBRA Cultural Network, which explores continuities and discontinuities in the cultural landscape between Flaeming and Duebener Heide in the district of Wittenberg (Saxony-Anhalt, Germany). National and international artists working in photography and film are invited to apply. The two 2026 residencies run in October 2026 in collaboration with KOBRA partners Ferropolis Foundation for Industrial Heritage and the Research Library of Reformation Studies Wittenberg (RFB). TOPIC ONE (Ferropolis): artistic perspectives on the history of the former Zschornewitz coal-fired power station, the transformation of industrial landscapes, and visions for the future of post-industrial areas. TOPIC TWO (RFB): exploring the research library as a store of knowledge and examining the production of knowledge and its visual representation, drawing on a collection of around 230,000 volumes including Reformation-era first editions, manuscripts and historical writings. ELIGIBILITY: minimum age 18, no age limit; students are not eligible. Artists are selected by jury on the quality of submitted work; selected artists notified from 20 June 2026. FUNDING per place: accommodation for the four-week stay, a EUR 5,000 grant, and an additional EUR 1,000 for childcare if attending with a child. OBLIGATIONS: four weeks present in October 2026, realisation of an artistic project, provision of ten project photographs by 31 December 2026, delivery of a one-day workshop for young people, a public artist talk, and two days participating in filming for a documentary. Artists retain copyright and ownership of works created. Childcare and child-friendly accommodation can be arranged; partners may also accompany the artist at the artist's own additional cost. No application fee. Apply via the online form at passageair.org by 30 May 2026; applications accepted in German or English.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Magnetic 5: Franco-UK Artist Residencies (2026 call)
item.description:
Organisation: Fluxus Art Projects

Location: France and United Kingdom (artists from a French region apply to the associated UK residency; artists from a UK nation apply to the associated French region)

Award: Threshold monthly fee of £2,100 / €2,500, plus accommodation, access to a workspace, curatorial mentoring and networking opportunities.

Duration: Two to three months (residency dates set per host)

Deadline: Closed 02 Jun 2026

Magnetic is a joint Franco-UK initiative coordinated by Fluxus Art Projects, bringing together ten institutions for a programme of 2-3 month artist residencies: five in France (Capc, Bordeaux; Frac Grand Large, Dunkirk; Frac Bretagne, Rennes; Villa Arson, Nice; Betonsalon, Paris) and five in the UK (Wysing Arts Centre and Gasworks in England; Flax Art Studios, Northern Ireland; Aberystwyth Arts Centre, Wales; Cove Park, Scotland). Artists from each of the five French regions apply for the associated UK residency; artists from each UK nation apply for the associated French region. Societal and environmental issues are at the heart of the programme; the residencies are situated, with selected artists expected to work in resonance with each host's geographical, historical, societal and artistic context.

Open call details

item.title: BrightBrain: Artist Residency 2026 (TU Braunschweig Science & Art Lab)
item.description:
Organisation: Science and Art Lab at TU Braunschweig, in cooperation with the BrightBrain research consortium

Location: Braunschweig, Germany (in-person residency at TU Braunschweig)

Award: Artist fee €2,400 per month (gross), plus travel costs and accommodation covered, plus production budget up to €3,500 (gross).

Duration: Autumn/winter 2026 residency (start from September 2026, exact start date flexible). Length agreed with host.

Deadline: Closed 25 May 2026

Open call for an international artist-in-residence at the Science and Art Lab at TU Braunschweig, in cooperation with the BrightBrain research consortium, as part of the Ecoversity initiative. The residency aims to connect artistic and scientific perspectives. Artists are invited to propose their own research-based projects related to microphotonics, biomedicine, technology, neurodegenerative diseases, neurobiology, neuromorphic computing, and microelectronics (the current resident is collaborating with the Nitride Technology Center on light transmission in digital applications including micro-LEDs and neuromorphic computing). Strong fit for art-science, sensory translation, critical-data, and neuroscience-informed practices. Eligibility: international artists; should show interest in biomedicine, technology and innovation in their artistic work; be comfortable working in interdisciplinary environments; be motivated to collaborate with experts from science, culture and education; be willing to participate in two public events during the residency; and be able to communicate in English. Application by PDF to artist-residency@tu-braunschweig.de with subject 'BrightBrain residency' (if files exceed 25 MB, upload via the TU Braunschweig Nextcloud link in the call). Application deadline 25 May 2026. Jury decision expected end of June 2026; residency starts from September 2026 (exact date flexible). Free to apply.

Open call details
